2605.28910.title: Hallucination Detection-Guided Preference Optimization for Clinical Summarization
2605.28910.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise on summarization tasks, but they often produce hallucinations, which are unsupported or incorrect statements that limit their reliability in specialized healthcare applications. We introduce \itermodelfull (\itermodel), an inference-time method that leverages hallucination detectors to guide iterative summary revisions toward factual corrections. Building on this, we propose \itermodel for Preference Learning (\model), which converts detector-guided refinement trajectories into preference pairs for model finetuning. Extensive experiments show that our methods substantially reduce hallucinations for Llama and Gemma models in summarizing real-world clinical notes from \MimicIV. For example, \itermodel reduces 24\% and \model reduces 48\% hallucinations in Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct. Importantly, both methods preserve summary fluency, coherence, and relevance according to human expert and LLM-Jury evaluations. Together, these results demonstrate that detection-informed refinement and preference learning offer an automated solution for improving factual faithfulness in clinical summarization.
2605.28910.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在摘要任務上顯示出潛力，但它們經常產生幻覺，即不支持或不正確的陳述，這限制了它們在專業醫療應用中的可靠性。<lb/>我們介紹了\itermodelfull（\itermodel），這是一種推理時方法，利用幻覺檢測器來指導迭代摘要修訂以達到事實修正。<lb/>在此基礎上，我們提出了\itermodel for Preference Learning（\model），它將檢測器引導的精煉軌跡轉換為模型微調的偏好對。<lb/>大量實驗表明，我們的方法顯著減少了Llama和Gemma模型在總結來自\MimicIV的真實臨床筆記時的幻覺。例如，\itermodel減少了Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct中的24\%幻覺，而\model減少了48\%幻覺。<lb/>重要的是，根據人類專家和LLM-Jury的評估，這兩種方法都保持了摘要的流暢性、一致性和相關性。<lb/>總體而言，這些結果表明，基於檢測的精煉和偏好學習提供了一種自動化解決方案，以提高臨床摘要中的事實真實性。
2605.28910.author: Shamanth Kuthpadi Seethakantha et.al.
2605.28910.authors: Shamanth Kuthpadi Seethakantha, Dung Ngoc Thai, Vara Prasad Gudi, Simran Tiwari, Rami Matar, Avijit Mitra, Wenlong Zhao, Wael Salloum, Andrew McCallum
